output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.

Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for kaggle_small_uber_eats_small, uber_eats_small_yelp_small, blocking on name_norm, city, state; `exact_match_multi` for kaggle_small_yelp_small, blocking on phone_e164, postal_code.
Performance-wise, pair completeness is 1.00 for all pairs and candidate counts range from 179 to 199972.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.75, f1=0.8947368421052632, f1=0.983050847457627, threshold=0.72, f1=0.9302325581395349); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was created to load the schema-matched parquet datasets for kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, and yelp_small and process all three pairwise matches.

It added pair-specific blocking with an EmbeddingBlocker for kaggle_small versus uber_eats_small, a StandardBlocker on phone_e164 and postal_code for kaggle_small versus yelp_small, and an EmbeddingBlocker for uber_eats_small versus yelp_small.
It defined rule-based matching for each dataset pair with explicit comparator sets, weights, and thresholds, then wrote the three correspondence outputs to the output/correspondences directory.
It concatenated all pairwise correspondences and added a fusion stage using DataFusionStrategy and DataFusionEngine to write fused results to output/data_fusion/fusion_data.csv.

The matching strategy uses a hybrid pair-specific approach instead of one uniform matcher, combining embedding-based candidate generation for text-driven pairs with exact-key standard blocking for the pair that shares phone and postal information.
The fusion strategy is strongly standardized around longest_string for most attributes, with only categories using union across sources.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

cluster analysis generated results for correspondences_kaggle_small_uber_eats_small.csv and correspondences_uber_eats_small_yelp_small.csv; flagged uber_eats_small↔yelp_small with recommended_action=update_matching_config; matching_config artifact was updated.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline adaptation.

The only concrete pipeline change in this run was a reweighting of the uber_eats_small to yelp_small rule-based matcher from [0.28, 0.16, 0.12, 0.16, 0.16, 0.12] to [0.38, 0.08, 0.08, 0.18, 0.18, 0.1].
The updated uber_eats_small to yelp_small matcher now places more emphasis on name_norm and geographic coordinates, while reducing the influence of street, city, and categories.
The matching behavior shifted only for the uber_eats_small and yelp_small pair toward stronger reliance on business name and latitude and longitude agreement.
The fusion behavior did not shift, because the same fusion strategy remained in place with longest_string used for 19 attributes and union used for categories.

The blocking setup was unchanged, including embedding blockers for kaggle_small to uber_eats_small and uber_eats_small to yelp_small, and a standard blocker on phone_e164 and postal_code for kaggle_small to yelp_small.
All three pairwise thresholds remained unchanged at 0.75 for kaggle_small to uber_eats_small, 0.75 for kaggle_small to yelp_small, and 0.72 for uber_eats_small to yelp_small.
Singleton retention remained disabled with include_singletons set to False.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 2; fusion_data.csv was produced.

pipeline.py changed only for uber_eats_small↔yelp_small RuleBasedMatcher weights: [0.28, 0.16, 0.12, 0.16, 0.16, 0.12] → [0.38, 0.08, 0.08, 0.18, 0.18, 0.1]; matching_config artifact was updated and matching_config_updated set; cluster analysis recorded correspondences_uber_eats_small_yelp_small.csv as unhealthy with one_to_many_ratio 0.0488, ambiguous_ratio 0.5, max_cluster_size 3, 3 size-3 clusters, recommended_action=update_matching_config, while kaggle_small↔uber_eats_small remained healthy.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline adaptation.
The only concrete pipeline change in this run was a reweighting of the uber_eats_small to yelp_small matcher from [0.38, 0.08, 0.08, 0.18, 0.18, 0.1] to [0.3, 0.14, 0.1, 0.2, 0.2, 0.06].
The blocking setup, comparator definitions, thresholds, correspondence exports, and fusion configuration were otherwise preserved from the prior pipeline.

The matching behavior for uber_eats_small versus yelp_small shifted away from heavy reliance on name similarity and category overlap toward relatively more emphasis on street, city, latitude, and longitude agreement.
The overall fusion behavior did not shift, because attribute fusers remained dominated by longest_string with union retained only for categories.
No thresholds were added, removed, or changed in this run.
No attribute fusers were added, removed, or changed in this run.
Singleton retention remained disabled with include_singletons set to False.
The fusion strategy still used longest_string for 19 attributes and union for the categories field.
The pipeline continued to use the same precomputed blockers, the same aligned parquet inputs, and the same output file paths.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 3; fusion_data.csv was produced.

pipeline.py changed only for rb_correspondences_uber_eats_small_yelp_small matcher weights: [0.38, 0.08, 0.08, 0.18, 0.18, 0.1] -> [0.3, 0.14, 0.1, 0.2, 0.2, 0.06]; matching_config artifact was updated; cluster_analysis_result flagged correspondences_uber_eats_small_yelp_small.csv recommended_action=update_matching_config after unhealthy clusters (50 total, max_cluster_size 11, one_to_many_ratio 0.3, ambiguous_ratio 0.5714); cluster summary was written with a new run_id.
This run produced an initial draft of the evaluation script for the current fused output.
The evaluation aligned fused records to gold IDs by deriving eval_id values from _fusion_sources or falling back to cluster _id values before running DataFusionEvaluator.

The strategy then assigned field-specific match functions, using tokenized matching for most textual attributes, numeric tolerance matching for numeric and coordinate attributes, and set equality matching for detected list-like columns.
Name, website, map URL, phone, address, locality, postal, country, and source fields were evaluated with tokenized_match.
House number, latitude, longitude, rating, and rating count were evaluated with numeric_tolerance_match.
Detected list-like non-system columns were normalized across fused and gold data and then evaluated with set_equality_match when no explicit function had already been assigned.
The run added helper functions to parse _fusion_sources values from JSON-like or Python-literal strings and to detect the dominant gold ID prefix.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 75.47%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The main failure in this run is incorrect fusion value selection rather than blocking failure, because matched clusters often exist but the fused attribute value is chosen poorly.
The current pipeline overuses longest_string for semantically different field types, which causes wrong outputs for URLs, normalized names, ratings, phones, coordinates, and provenance fields.
A secondary problem is record-level coverage loss because include_singletons is set to false, so unmatched records can disappear entirely from fused output.
Overall accuracy is 0.7546933667083855 across 799 evaluations, with 603 correct values over 40 evaluated records and 21 evaluated attributes.
Map URL accuracy is 0.4, and the analysis states that fused values are usually Google Maps URLs while the gold data expects Yelp URLs.
Name normalization accuracy is 0.875, and the analysis reports that fusion picks longer variants such as "the sycamore restaurant" instead of the expected "the sycamore".
Rating accuracy is 0.7, and the analysis states that fused outputs often select a lower or older alternative rating.
Phone raw accuracy is 0.0 and phone E.164 accuracy is 0.0, which strongly indicates formatting or type normalization problems.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was newly drafted using the provided schema-matching outputs for kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, and yelp_small.

Blocking and matching were configured with the supplied pair-specific blockers, comparators, weights, and thresholds of 0.75 for kaggle_small to uber_eats_small, 0.75 for kaggle_small to yelp_small, and 0.72 for uber_eats_small to yelp_small.
A data fusion stage was added with longest_string assigned to 19 attributes and union assigned to categories.
The fused output was produced from concatenated correspondences across all three dataset pairs and written to output/data_fusion/fusion_data.csv.
The fusion behavior in this draft is dominated by generic longest_string selection rather than attribute-specific or type-aware value selection.
Categories were fused by set-style aggregation with union, while nearly every other field was resolved by choosing the longest textual value.
Matching behavior remained based on the precomputed pair-specific rules, with the most permissive threshold applied to the uber_eats_small to yelp_small pair at 0.72.
Singleton retention was disabled by setting include_singletons to False, so unmatched records are excluded from fused output.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

cluster analysis produced results for correspondences_kaggle_small_uber_eats_small.csv: healthy long-tail, 58 clusters, max_cluster_size 2, small_cluster_ratio 1.0, large_cluster_ratio 0.0, one_to_many_ratio 0.0, ambiguous_ratio 0.0, mean score 0.8169, p90 0.906, meets_target=true; correspondences_uber_eats_small_yelp_small.csv: recommended_action=update_matching_config; matching_config artifact was updated; cluster_summary_path/run_id written; effect: next node remains PENDING.
This run was a refinement of the evaluation logic in evaluation_refinement mode rather than an initial draft or a repair.

The dominant strategy was to refine evaluation alignment by mapping fused records to gold ids through parsed fusion sources and corrected gold-prefix detection while preserving field-specific evaluators such as tokenized matching, numeric tolerance matching, and set equality for list-like columns.

Identifier-alignment behavior was tightened by changing gold prefix parsing from underscore-based splitting to hyphen-based splitting and by allowing cluster-id fallback only when the cluster id exists in the gold id set.
Schema-handling behavior was improved for gold inputs by renaming a gold _id column to id when needed before evaluation.
List-like fields continued to be detected and normalized jointly across fused and gold data, with non-underscore-prefixed list columns evaluated using set equality.
Text fields such as name, name_norm, website, map_url, phone_raw, phone_e164, address lines, street, city, state, postal_code, country, and source continued to use tokenized_match.
Numeric-like fields such as house_number, latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count continued to use numeric_tolerance_match.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 51.69%; previous logged accuracy was 75.47%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The run is primarily failing because fused output coverage is incomplete, causing many gold entities to be absent or unmapped in evaluation and producing missing fused values.
The run is secondarily failing because schema and preprocessing mismatches are degrading attribute accuracy, especially for phone, street, source, map_url, and _id fields.
The run is also affected by ambiguous uber_eats_small to yelp_small clusters that can contaminate fused values through over-merged entities.
Overall accuracy is 0.5168961201501877 and macro accuracy is 0.5206393083549207 across 40 evaluated records and 799 evaluations.
Many debug mismatches reportedly show fused_id as NaN with reason equal to missing_fused_value for fields including street, latitude, and phone_raw.
The current fusion engine is run with include_singletons set to False.
The analysis states that unmatched test entities are excluded from fused output entirely when include_singletons is False, which explains widespread missing fused values.
Several field accuracies are extremely low, including phone_raw_accuracy at 0.0, phone_e164_accuracy at 0.0, source_accuracy at 0.0, _id_accuracy at 0.0, and map_url_accuracy at 0.125.
This run was an evaluation-driven refinement pass rather than an initial draft or a repair with implemented code changes.
The before and after pipelines remained identical in this run.
No concrete pipeline changes were applied in this run.

Singleton retention remained disabled with include_singletons set to False.

The analysis also recommended making fusion more conservative around ambiguous Uber Eats to Yelp clusters, but matching thresholds and correspondence handling stayed unchanged.

This run was a refinement of the evaluation script in evaluation_refinement mode rather than an initial draft or repair.

The dominant strategy was to refine field-specific evaluation behavior by explicitly assigning comparison functions based on data shape and semantics while keeping the existing eval-view alignment and normalization flow.

The run kept tokenized_match for text-like scalar fields including name, name_norm, website, map_url, phone_raw, phone_e164, address_line1, address_line2, street, city, state, postal_code, country, and source.
The run kept numeric_tolerance_match for numeric or tolerance-sensitive fields including house_number, latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count.
The run newly assigned categories to set_equality_match explicitly, aligning it with list-like or set-valued comparison behavior.
The run continued to register set_equality_match for detected list-like columns that were not already assigned a function.
The only code change shown was the addition of register_eval("categories", set_equality_match, "set_equality_match").
The run preserved helper logic for parsing _fusion_sources, detecting gold ID prefixes, and building an eval view that maps fused rows to gold eval_id values.
